question_text: A student heats ice and measures its temperature every 30 seconds. For the first 5 minutes, the temperature stays at 0°C even though heat energy is being added. Explain why the temperature does not change during this time.
correct_answer: The heat energy is being used to break the bonds between particles (or to overcome the forces holding particles in fixed positions) rather than making the particles move faster. This energy is called latent heat of fusion. Once all the ice has melted, the extra heat energy will increase the temperature of the liquid water.
explanation: During melting, heat energy goes into changing the state of matter (breaking the fixed arrangement), not into increasing kinetic energy (temperature). This is the concept of latent heat. The temperature only rises again after the change of state is complete and all the ice has become water.
